headline.biggest_gap_line: Multi-Agent / Tool-Use Capability vs Scalable Oversight (cap +21% / saf -6%)
headline.meaning: The defining tension this quarter is autonomous agents—coding agents, embodied multi-agent systems, and self-evolving design loops—scaling faster than the oversight and monitoring machinery meant to catch their failures.
headline.why_it_matters: When agents that individually pass tests still accumulate ecosystem-level harms no single contribution accounts for, per-agent benchmarks and external perimeter defenses miss the actual risk surface, and oversight that contracts (-5.6%) while agentic capability surges (+38.6%) widens that blind spot.

overall_foresight.risk: California SB 53 incident-reporting and frontier risk frameworks are being authored by deployment teams against a safety-research base (scalable oversight, deceptive alignment) that is itself losing velocity and rising in critical-uncertainty share — freezing a contested methodology into a mandatory compliance artifact.

Agentic Behavior Monitoring is scaling fast (123.5/qtr) precisely as PRC-linked influence operations probe the data-center/electricity-cost narrative — meaning agent-monitoring telemetry becomes a high-value, under-secured surveillance target whose compromise reveals both operational patterns and grid-siting sensitivities.

AI rare-disease/genomic diagnosis pipelines are being deployed via accelerating agentic capability into a clinical setting where the safety subfield governing exactly that failure mode — Scalable Oversight — is shrinking in velocity while its critical-uncertainty share rises, meaning the verification methods clinicians implicitly trust are quietly becoming less validated, not more.

weekly_foresight.risk: AI fraud/claim-verification pipelines built by detection engineers are silently adopting 'deception' constructs from the alignment literature, importing alignment-research immaturity into consequential financial and information-gatekeeping decisions.

Formal-guarantee control papers (PAC-Bayesian certificates) are being filed under safety topics, creating a 'certified-safe' vocabulary that could be invoked to satisfy SB 53 / EU AI Act risk-framework obligations for systems whose guarantees only hold under assumptions that don't survive deployment.
